message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Workshop Kitchen + Bar (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,596 reviews; Google 1,250+ reviews; OpenTable 1,788 ratings; Tripadvisor 831 reviews — aggregate 5,000+ across platforms
- Sentiment: mostly positive with notable mixed signal (Yelp/Tripadvisor reviews split between exceptional and disappointed; Google 4.3 and OpenTable 4.4 anchor positive)
- Longevity: Operating since October 2012 (13+ years); original chef/owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani still attached per current site — ownership stable
- Credentials: ["James Beard Foundation Award — Outstanding Restaurant Design, 75+ seats (2015, awarded to SOMA Architects for the space)","Michelin Guide recommended — multi-year (described as 4-time Michelin recommended; Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025)","Restaurant & Bar Design Awards — Best Design for the Americas (2013)","Featured by Palm Springs Life, Hotels Above Par, Time Out Palm Springs and regional best-of fine-dining lists"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Michelin restaurants Palm Springs', 'James Beard Palm Springs', 'fine dining Palm Springs', and 'best restaurants Palm Springs uptown design district'. Less reliably surfaced in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' agent rollups, which tend to lead with Le Vallauris, The Steakhouse (Agua Caliente), Edge Steakhouse, Copley's, and Wally's. Does not surface for cuisine-specific queries like 'New American near Palm Springs' as consistently as its reputation would suggest — agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's for that bracket.
- Site structured data: Weak. workshopkitchenbar.com presents address, hours, and menu in human-readable text only. No schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup detected, no clear Open Graph or microdata. Hours wording ('Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm') is ambiguous for parsers. Resy handles reservations off-site. Strong human design, weak agent-readable signal.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's the information about Workshop Kitchen Bar's James Beard Award:

Workshop Kitchen Bar, located in the historic El Paseo Building on North Palm Canyon in Palm Springs, became Palm Springs' first James Beard award winner for Outstanding Restaurant Design in the category of over 75 seats. The international architecture firm SOMA Architects received the James Beard Award for best Restaurant Design or Renovation in North America for their design of Workshop Kitchen & Bar in 2015.

The restaurant was completed in October of 2012 and was designed for chef owners Michael Beckman and Joe Mourani. Designer Michel Abboud of SOMA Architects designed the interior.

The James Beard Awards gala was held at the Lyric Opera of Chicago on May 4th, making this a significant recognition for the restaurant's innovative design.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen Bar and its Michelin Guide recognition:

Workshop Kitchen & Bar is a restaurant in the 2025 MICHELIN Guide USA, and it earns consecutive Michelin Plates (2024–2025) for Californian cuisine in Palm Springs.

The restaurant is a 4-time Michelin recommended & James Beard awarded restaurant in Palm Springs. Located in the heart of Palm Springs' Uptown Design District, Workshop Kitchen & Bar offers an unforgettable dining experience in a relaxed yet sophisticated setting.

The restaurant is curated by Executive Chef Michael Beckman, and features farm-to-table cuisine with locally sourced ingredients. Additionally, Workshop Kitchen + Bar has received a James Beard Award for its brutalist interior design.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen Bar in Palm Springs and its design awards:

Workshop Kitchen and Bar opened on September 22, 2012 and in 2013 the London-based Restaurant & Bar Design Awards named Workshop "Best Design for the Americas". The project won Best Designed Restaurant for the Americas region in the international 2013 Restaurant & Bar Design Awards.

The restaurant also received additional recognition: an "Outstanding Restaurant Design, 76 Seats & Over" win in 2015 from the James Beard Foundation.

Workshop Kitchen + Bar is located in Palm Springs, CA and SOMA created the "industrial chic" aesthetic, juxtaposing modern accents with old structures, mixing warm, raw materials with clean lines and stark colors. The restaurant is housed in a 1926 Spanish Colonial-style space which boasts 3,700 square feet and 27-foot-high ceilings.

input.prompt: Who are the current owners/chef? Does it mention Michael Beckman and Joseph Mourani? When did it open? What awards are listed?

content.content:
# Workshop Kitchen & Bar Leadership & Awards

**Current Ownership & Leadership:**
Yes, the page confirms both individuals are involved. Michael Beckman serves as "Executive Chef / Co-Owner," while Joseph Mourani is listed as "Co-Owner" (with a "Coming Soon" bio section).

**Opening Date:**
The restaurant "opened on September 22, 2012."

**Awards Listed:**
- 2013: London-based Restaurant & Bar Design Awards named Workshop "Best Design for the Americas"
- 2015: James Beard Foundation recognized it for "Outstanding Restaurant Design, 76 Seats & Over"
- Michelin Recommended status (indicated by imagery and branding throughout)

The page emphasizes that both founders "met while studying at the Paul Bocuse Institute in Lyon, France" and "partnered together to repurpose the 1926 historic El Paseo building in the Palm Springs Design District."

Web search results for query: ""Workshop Kitchen" Palm Springs Michael Beckman Joseph Mourani owner 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Workshop Kitchen + Bar - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Workshop_Kitchen_+_Bar"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen | Michelin Recommended Restaurant Palm Springs","url":"https://www.workshopkitchenbar.com/"},{"title":"Workshopping Palm Springs :: All Things Andy Gavin","url":"https://all-things-andy-gavin.com/2025/06/18/workshopping-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Palm Springs | Workshop Kitchen+Bar","url":"https://www.workshopkitchenbar.com/?p=rose"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen + Bar Now Open in Palm Springs | Chilled Magazine","url":"https://chilledmagazine.com/workshop-kitchen-bar-now-open-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Dining Around the Desert: Workshop Kitchen + Bar, Palm Springs - Sheri Dettman and Associates","url":"https://www.yourresorthome.com/dining-around-the-desert-workshop-kitchen-bar-palm-springs/"},{"title":"WORKSHOP KITCHEN & BAR - Updated September 2025 - 1413 Photos & 1541 Reviews - 800 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - New American - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/workshop-kitchen-and-bar-palm-springs-2?start=100"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen | Palm Springs Catering & Events","url":"https://www.workshopkitchencatering.com/"},{"title":"Palm Springs - architecture | Workshop Kitchen + Bar Becomes Palm Springs' 1st James Beard Award Winner for Design","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/workshop-kitchen-bar-becomes-palm-springs-1st-james-beard-award-winner-for-design/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen + Bar by SOMA - Best Designed Restaurant in the Americas","url":"https://bustler.net/news/3148/workshop-kitchen-bar-by-soma-best-designed-restaurant-in-the-americas"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen in Palm Springs:

Workshop Kitchen + Bar is a restaurant and bar located in Palm Springs, California. The restaurant is owned by Michael Beckman and Joseph Mourani.

Chef Michael Beckman and Chef Joe Mourani met while studying at the Paul Bocuse Institute in Lyon, France. In 2012, they partnered together to repurpose the 1926 historic El Paseo building in the Palm Springs Design District.

The restaurant has received significant recognition. The 2015 James Beard Award-winning restaurant features a market-driven menu comprised of seasonal food highlights harvested from local farms. The Michelin Guide has described the cuisine as Californian, and Fodor's says the menu is American.

SOMA Architects carved a soaring, almost Brutalist dining room out of the old structure: 27-foot concrete walls, cathedral-like niches of black leather, a 35-foot communal table of poured concrete, and an open, copper-clad kitchen that glows like a forge.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: All major credentials confirmed. Note: opening date is September 22, 2012, not October 2012 as claimed. Let me verify Michelin specifically.

Links: [{"title":"Workshop Kitchen | Michelin Recommended Restaurant Palm Springs","url":"https://www.workshopkitchenbar.com/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen & Bar, Palm Springs | En Primeur Club","url":"https://www.enprimeurclub.com/restaurants/workshop-kitchen-bar-palm-springs-restaurant"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen & Bar – Palm Springs - a MICHELIN Guide Restaurant","url":"https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/california/palm-springs/restaurant/workshop-kitchen-bar"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen & Bar in Palm Springs | Michelin-Recommended Dining","url":"https://www.hotelsabovepar.com/articles/restaurants/workshop-kitchen-bar-palm-springs-michelin-restaurant"},{"title":"8 Palm Springs Restaurants Recognized in Michelin Guide | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/8-palm-springs-restaurants-recognized-in-michelin-guide/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen | Michelin Star Restaurant Palm Springs","url":"https://www.workshopkitchenbar.com/?pl=visitpalmsprings"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen and Bar","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/eat-and-drink/workshop-kitchen-and-bar/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen & Bar","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/workshop-kitchen-%26-bar/11/"},{"title":"WORKSHOP KITCHEN & BAR, Palm Springs - Menu, Prices & Restaurant Reviews - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32847-d3562210-Reviews-Workshop_Kitchen_Bar-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen + Bar","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/workshop-kitchen-+-bar/11/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen in Palm Springs and its Michelin recognition in 2024:

Workshop Kitchen & Bar on North Palm Canyon Drive earns consecutive Michelin Plates (2024–2025) for Californian cuisine in Palm Springs. The Plate designation marks kitchens that Michelin's inspectors consider worth visiting without yet rising to the star tier, and in a market the size of Palm Springs, sustaining it across two consecutive guides means the kitchen is performing consistently rather than on a single strong season.

The restaurant features farm-to-table food with nearby Coachella Valley produce, supported by a dedicated garden in Rancho Mirage that furnishes peak-season produce for wood-fired dishes. The setting is a Spanish-style building from the 1920s, while the interior is a modernist temple of polished concrete—a mist-cooled courtyard provides additional seating.

Additionally, Workshop Kitchen is a 4-time Michelin recommended & James Beard awarded restaurant in Palm Springs.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I have enough verification. Note one minor discrepancy on opening date (September 22, 2012, not October 2012 as claimed).

confirms: James Beard Foundation Award 2015 — Outstanding Restaurant Design (75+ seats), awarded to SOMA Architects for Workshop Kitchen + Bar — confirmed via Palm Springs Life, PR Newswire, and SOMA press release; Palm Springs' first JBF design winner.
Restaurant & Bar Design Awards 2013 — Best Designed Restaurant for the Americas region — confirmed via Bustler, Trendland, and Palm Springs Life.

Michelin Guide recommended — Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025 confirmed (consecutive Plate designations for Californian cuisine); listed on guide.michelin.com (403 on direct fetch but corroborated across multiple sources including Palm Springs Life's '8 Palm Springs Restaurants Recognized in Michelin Guide').
Ownership stable — Michael Beckman (Executive Chef/Co-Owner) and Joseph Mourani (Co-Owner) confirmed on current workshopkitchenbar.com About page; met at Paul Bocuse Institute in Lyon.
Longevity / 13+ years operating — confirmed opening in 2012, currently active in 2026.
Featured by Palm Springs Life and Hotels Above Par — confirmed via direct hits in search results.
contradicts: Opening month: claim states 'Operating since October 2012'; multiple independent sources (workshopkitchenbar.com About page, Palm Springs Life) state the restaurant opened September 22, 2012 — not October. Minor but factually off.
flagged_as_unverified: '4-time Michelin recommended' specific count — restaurant's own marketing and aggregator sites repeat the '4-time' phrasing, but I could not independently enumerate four distinct guide years from primary Michelin Guide source (guide.michelin.com returned 403). 2024 and 2025 Plates are confirmed; the other two years (likely 2019, 2021, or 2022 guides) are not independently verified.
Time Out Palm Springs feature — not surfaced in independent searches.

James Beard nomination/award for the restaurant or chef directly (beyond the SOMA design award) — none found, and the claim only attributes the award to SOMA, which is correct. No conflicting evidence.
